feed.title: AiRA-HUST
feed.subtitle: An engaging 1-3 sentence description of your lab.

entry.title: Invited Lecture – Artificial Intelligence for Inverse Designs

link.title: Invited Lecture – Artificial Intelligence for Inverse Designs

entry.content:
AiRA Lab is honored to deliver an invited lecture titled “Artificial Intelligence for Inverse Designs” as part of the student exchange and collaboration program between Shibaura Institute of Technology (SIT), Japan and Hanoi University of Science and Technology (HUST).

This lecture introduces how modern AI methods – including deep learning, surrogate modeling, and optimization under uncertainty – can be used to automatically design engineering structures and systems that satisfy complex performance requirements. We discuss applications ranging from mechanical components to smart structures, where AI helps search extremely high-dimensional design spaces that are intractable for traditional approaches.

The event is hosted within a broader program that combines design projects, cultural exchange, and joint workshops between SIT and HUST. It provides a unique opportunity for students to learn about cutting-edge AI techniques while experiencing international collaboration in engineering education.
entry.summary: AiRA Lab is honored to deliver an invited lecture titled “Artificial Intelligence for Inverse Designs” as part of the student exchange and collaboration program between Shibaura Institute of Technology (SIT), Japan and Hanoi University of Science and Technology (HUST).

entry.title: AiRA Lab Presents at ICEMA-25 Conference

link.title: AiRA Lab Presents at ICEMA-25 Conference

entry.content:
We are excited to announce that AiRA Lab is presenting our latest research at the International Conference on Engineering Mechanics and Applications (ICEMA-25), held at Vietnam National University (VNU), Hanoi on November 14, 2025.

Our team presented three research papers showcasing cutting-edge work in computational mechanics and dynamic systems.

Publications at ICEMA-25

  1. Trong, G. N., Truong-Quoc, C., & Nguyen Quang, H. (2025). High-Fidelity Modeling of the Flexible Inverted Pendulum via Pseudo-Rigid-Body (PRB). Proceedings of the 8th International Conference on Engineering Mechanics and Automation (ICEMA 2025), Hanoi, November 14-15, 2025. DOI: 10.15625/vap.2026.0008

  2. Ngoc, V. L. N., Truong-Quoc, C., & Nguyen-Thai, M.-T. (2025). Exploring PD Control Effects on the Basins of Attraction in Segway Dynamics. Proceedings of the 8th International Conference on Engineering Mechanics and Automation (ICEMA 2025), Hanoi, November 14-15, 2025. DOI: 10.15625/vap.2026.0011

  3. Nhu, T. N., Truong-Quoc, C., & Thai Phuong, T. (2025). Dynamic Behavior of Kresling Origami under Harmonic Excitation: A Five-Parameter Energy Framework. Proceedings of the 8th International Conference on Engineering Mechanics and Automation (ICEMA 2025), Hanoi, November 14-15, 2025. DOI: 10.15625/vap.2026.0031

The conference brings together researchers, engineers, and industry professionals to discuss the latest advances in engineering mechanics, computational methods, and their applications across various fields.

This presentation represents our ongoing commitment to bridging fundamental research with practical applications, demonstrating how AI and computational methods can revolutionize traditional engineering approaches.

We look forward to engaging with the international research community and exploring new collaborative opportunities that emerge from this prestigious conference.
entry.summary: We are excited to announce that AiRA Lab is presenting our latest research at the International Conference on Engineering Mechanics and Applications (ICEMA-25), held at Vietnam National University (VNU), Hanoi on November 14, 2025.
